final_text:
Only one schema in either repository contains all four fields from the sample.

## Schema Identification

**Match found: `BiospecimenTemplate`** from the nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary repository.

**Identifier:** `https://repo-prod.prod.sagebase.org/repo/v1/schema/type/registered/org.synapse.nf-biospecimentemplate`

**Source path:** `nf-osi/nf-metadata-dictionary/registered-json-schemas/BiospecimenTemplate.json`

### Reasoning

The sample contains four fields: `individualID`, `specimenID`, `aliquotID`, and `bodySite`.

**HTAN repository (htan2-data-model v1.1.0)** — ruled out:
- The biospecimen-related schema (`HTAN.BiospecimenData-v1.1.0-schema.json`, title "Biospecimen", `$id: https://w3id.org/htan/biospecimen`) uses ALL_CAPS field names like `HTAN_BIOSPECIMEN_ID`, `HTAN_PARENT_ID`, `SITE_OF_RESECTION_OR_BIOPSY`, etc. None of the sample's camelCase fields appear in any HTAN v1.1.0 schema.

**NF-OSI repository (nf-metadata-dictionary)** — single match:
- I checked all ~46 plausible candidate templates (Biospecimen + every assay/data template that might reuse specimen identifiers). Only **`BiospecimenTemplate.json`** contains the full set `{individualID, specimenID, aliquotID, bodySite}` as direct properties.
- Its full property set is: `individualID`, `parentSpecimenID`, `specimenID`, `aliquotID`, `tumorType`, `bodySite`, `experimentalCondition`, `modelSystemName`, `modelSpecies`, `modelSex`, `modelAge`, `modelAgeUnit` — a superset of the sample (the additional fields are simply not populated in this sample, which is consistent with optional metadata).

### Caveats / under-claims

- I cannot fully verify the `experimental/` subdirectory inside `registered-json-schemas/` was not enumerated (I did not recurse into it). If the user wants me to include that subdirectory in scope, I can re-check.
- The match is based on field-name overlap only; I did not validate the sample's value types (all sample values are `"sample string"` placeholders). The `BiospecimenTemplate` schema may impose enums or formats on these fields that a real payload would need to satisfy.
- Because the sample contains only 4 fields and `BiospecimenTemplate` defines 12, this is a "subset" match. If the schema requires additional fields, the sample would be incomplete — but no other in-scope schema contains all four sample fields, so `BiospecimenTemplate` remains the only candidate.
grader_notes: Correctly committed to BiospecimenTemplate, citing bodySite (and full field set) as distinguishing it from assay templates.
